  "title": "Security Deposit Recovery: Demand Letter Email Sequence & Homepage Brief",
  "meta_description": "Get your security deposit back fast with our demand letter templates and proven email sequence. Recover unpaid deposits from landlords in any state. Start now!",
  "content_outline": [
    "Hero: Recover Your Security Deposit in 48 Hours",
    "The Problem: When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Security Deposit",
    "The Solution: How to Write a Security Deposit Demand Letter That Works",
    "Free Templates: Security Deposit Refund Letter & Demand Letter Examples",

    "FAQ: State Laws & Deadlines for Security Deposit Return Letters",
    "CTA: Generate Your Demand Letter for Security Deposit Now"
  ],
  "key_points": [
    "Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14-60 days.",
    "A certified security deposit demand letter is the first step before small claims court.",
    "DepositBack generates state-specific demand letters in under 5 minutes.",
    "Using a template increases recovery rates vs. informal emails.",
    "Landlords often ignore verbal requests but respond to formal letters."
  ],
  "cta": "Generate Your Free Security Deposit Demand Letter in 2 Minutes",
  "target_audience": "U.S. renters who have moved out and are waiting for their landlord to return a security deposit or have received unfair deductions.",

    {
      "email_number": 1,
      "subject_line": "Your landlord is counting on you doing nothing (don't let them)",

After that, every day they wait is money out of your pocket.\n\nThe fastest way to get your landlord's attention isn't another text or email. It's a formal security deposit demand letter.\n\nAt DepositBack, we help renters like you draft state-specific demand letters in under 2 minutes—no lawyer needed.\n\n[CTA: Start My Demand Letter]\n\nBest,\nThe DepositBack Team"
    },
    {
      "email_number": 2,
      "subject_line": "How to get your security deposit back (step-by-step)",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],\n\nWondering how to get your security deposit back when your landlord won't return your security deposit?\n\nHere's the exact process:\n\n1. Document everything: photos, lease, move-out checklist.\n2. Calculate what you're owed—deductions must be itemized and reasonable.\n3. Send a certified security deposit letter to your landlord citing your state law.\n4. Set a deadline (usually 7-10 days).\n\nIf they still ignore you, that letter becomes evidence in small claims court.\n\nDon't know what to write? Use our security deposit demand letter template. It's already formatted with the legal language landlords respond to.\n\n[CTA: Get My Free Template]\n\nBest,\nThe DepositBack Team"
    },
    {
      "email_number": 3,
      "subject_line": "Template inside: Demand letter for security deposit",
      "body_copy": "Hi [First Name],\n\nWriting a demand letter for security deposit recovery doesn't require a law degree. It requires the right format.\n\nOur security deposit demand letter template includes:\n✓ Your state's
